corrections_by_claim.note: The article discusses R&D success in the chip industry, but does not analyze whether this is productive capital investment in a capitalist sense or rent-seeking/moat-building. It is a geopolitical report, not a political-economy analysis.
No engagement with the specific profit mechanisms of search or data extraction.

The article describes the state as a regulator of exports, which is a standard geopolitical function. It does not provide evidence of the state's constitutive role in the internal capital accumulation or market dominance of specific firms (e.g., through procurement or funding) as required by C3.
No engagement with profit rates or the rent-vs-profit distinction.
No balance-sheet analysis of tangible vs. intangible assets.
No engagement with the theoretical framework of expropriation vs. exploitation.
